title: Night
content:
В В В В The night is young yet; an enchanted night
В В В В In early summer: calm and darkly bright.

В В В В I love the Night, and every little breeze
В В В В She brings, to soothe the sleep of dreaming trees.

В В В В Hearst thou the Voices? Sough! Susurrus! Hark!
В В В В пїЅTis Mother Nature whispering in the dark!

В В В В Burden of cities, mad turmoil of men,
В В В В That vex the daylight, she forgets them then.

В В В В Her breasts are bare; Grief gains from them surcease:
В В В В She gives her restless sons the milk of Peace.

В В В В To sleep she lulls them, drawn from thoughts of pelf
В В В В By telling sweet old stories of herself.

В В В В .В В В В  .В В В В  .В В В В  .В В В В  .

В В В В All secrets deep, yea, all I hear and see
В В В В Of things mysterious, Night reveals to me.

В В В В I know what every flower, with drowsy head
В В В В Down-drooping, dreams of, and the seeming dead.

В В В В I know how they, escaped from care and strife,
В В В В Ironically moralise on Life.

В В В В And know what, when the moon walks on the waves
В В В В They whisper to each other in their graves.

В В В В I know that white clouds drifting from stark coasts
В В В В Across the sky at midnight are the ghosts

В В В В Of sailors drowned at sea, who yearn to win
В В В В A quiet grave beside their kith and kin

В В В В In still green graveyards, where they lie at ease
В В В В Far from the sound of surge and roar of seas.

В В В В I know the message of the mournful rain
В В В В That beats upon the widowпїЅs window-pane.

В В В В I know the meaning of the roar of seas;
В В В В I know the glad Spring sap-song of the trees;

В В В В And that great chant to which in tuneful grooves
В В В В The green round earth upon its axis moves;

В В В В And that still greater chant the Bright Sun sings,
В В В В Fire-crowned Apollo, the great chant that brings

В В В В All things to life, and draws through spaces dim,
В В В В And star-sown realms, his planets after him.

В В В В I know the tune that led, since Life began,
В В В В The upward, downward, onward March of Man.

В В В В I hear the whispers that the Angels twain
В В В В Of Death and Life exchange in meeting, fain

В В В В Are they to pause and greet, yet may not stay.
В В В В пїЅNever!пїЅ пїЅFor ever.пїЅ This is all they say.

В В В В I hear the twitterings inarticulate
В В В В Of souls unborn that press around the Gate

В В В В Of Birth, each striving which shall first escape
В В В В From formless vapour into human shape.

В В В В I know the tale the bird of passionate heart,
В В В В The nightingale, tries ever to impart

В В В В To men, though vainly, for I well believe
В В В В That in her brown breast beats the heart of Eve,

В В В В Who with her sweet, sad, wistful music tries
В В В В To tell her sons of their lost Paradise,

В В В В And solemn secrets Man had grace to know,
В В В В When God walked in the Garden long ago.

В В В В .В В В В  .В В В В  .В В В В  .В В В В  .

В В В В Yea, I have seen, methought, on nights of awe,
В В В В The vision terrible Lucretius saw:

В В В В The trembling Universe, suns, stars, grief, bliss,
В В В В Plunging for ever down a black abyss.

В В В В But more I love good Bishop Jeremy,
В В В В Who likens all the star-worlds that we see.

В В В В Which seem to run an everlasting race,
В В В В Unto a snowstorm sweeping on through space.

В В В В Suns, planets, stars, in glorious array
В В В В They march, melodious, on their unknown way.

В В В В Thought, seraph-winged and swifter than the light,
В В В В Unto the dim verge of the Infinite,

В В В В Pursues them, through that strange ethereal flood
В В В В In which they swim (mayhap it is the blood

В В В В Of Universal God wherein they are
В В В В But corpuscles, sun, satellite, and star.

В В В В And their great stream of glory but a dim,
В В В В Small pulse in the remotest vein of Him)

В В В В Pursues in vain, and from lone, awful glooms
В В В В Turns back to earth again with weary plumes.

В В В В .В В В В  .В В В В  .В В В В  .В В В В  .

В В В В Through glacial gulfs of Space the soul must roam
В В В В To feel the comfort of its earthly home.

В В В В Ah, Mother dear! broad-bosomed Mother Earth!
В В В В Mother of all our Joy, Grief, Madness, Mirth!

В В В В Mother of flower and fruit, of stream and sea!
В В В В We are thy children and must cling to thee.

В В В В I lay my head upon thy breast and hear,
В В В В Small, small and faint, yet strangely sweet and clear.

В В В В The hum and clash of little worlds below,
В В В В Each on its own path moving, swift or slow.

В В В В And listening, ever with intenter ear,
В В В В Through din of wars invisible I hear

В В В В A Homer, genius is not gauged by mass,
В В В В Singing his Iliad on a blade of grass.

В В В В And nations hearken: his great song resounds
В В В В Unto the tussockпїЅs very utmost bounds.

В В В В States rise and fall, each blade of grass upon,
В В В В But still his song from blade to blade rolls on

В В В В Through all the tussock-world, and Helen still
В В В В Is Fairest Fair, and Ajax wild of will.

В В В В An Ajax whose huge size, when measured oпїЅer,
В В В В Is full ten-thousandth of an inch or more.

В В В В Still hurls defiance at the gods whose home
В В В В Is in the distant, awful, dew-drop dome

В В В В That trembling hangs, suspended from a spray
В В В В An inch above him, worlds of space away.

В В В В Old prophecies foretell, but Time proves all,
В В В В The day will come when it, like Troy, shall fall.

В В В В Lo! through this small great wondrous song there runs
В В В В The marching melody of stars and suns.

В В В В .В В В В  .В В В В  .В В В В  .В В В В  .

В В В В I know these things, yet cannot speak and tell
В В В В Their meanings. Over all is cast a spell.

В В В В Secrets they are, sealed with a sevenfold seal;
В В В В My soul knows what my tongue may not reveal.

В В В В .В В В В  .В В В В  .В В В В  .В В В В  .

В В В В I love the Night! Bright Day the soul shuts in;
В В В В Night sends it soaring to its starry kin.

В В В В If I must leave at last my place of birth,
В В В В This homely, gracious, green, familiar Earth,

В В В В With all it holds of sorrow and delight,
В В В В I pray my parting-hour may be at night,

В В В В And that her curtain dark may softly fall
В В В В On scenes I love, ere I depart from all.

В В В В Then shall I haply, journeying through the Vast
В В В В Mysterious Silences, take one long, last

В В В В Fond look at Earth, and watch from depths afar
В В В В The dear old planet dwindling to a star;

В В В В And sigh farewell unto the friends of yore,
В В В В Whose kindly faces I shall see no more.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Victor James Daley, titled "Night", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...
